frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: novel objective balancing accuracy against spiking cost in recurrent circuits

frameworks.name: gain-adaptive recurrent network model
frameworks.one_line: gain modulation propagates through recurrent network to yield fast tuning adaptation

frameworks.name: prior-attraction vs adapter-repulsion accounts
frameworks.one_line: reconciles tuning-curve repulsion with predicted prior attraction

core_question: Can efficient-coding tuning curves adapt rapidly to changing sensory statistics, and how do gain-modulated recurrent dynamics produce observed adapter repulsion versus prior attraction?
key_assumptions: neural gains optimize an accuracy-vs-spiking-cost objective
tuning curves are shaped by gains propagating through recurrent connections
sensory environments change and require ongoing adaptation
key_findings: model reproduces subtle adapter-repulsion under peaked priors
model predicts fast prior attraction under broader distributions
behavioral evidence supports the predicted prior attraction
interpretations: gain modulation in recurrent circuits unifies adapter-repulsion and prior-attraction phenomena
rapid efficient coding is mechanistically realizable via gain propagation
